265	\section{The substrate \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}.\mathtt{tensorObj}\)}
266	\label{sec:tensorobj_substrate}
267	
268	\begin{definition}
269	
270	\leanok
271	[The substrate operation $\otimes$ on $\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X$]
272	  \label{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj}
274	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. For
275	  \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), define
276	  \[
277	    M \otimes_X N \;\;\in\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
278	  \]
279	  as follows. Choose an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}_{i \in I}\)
280	  of \(X\). On each \(U_i\), the restriction
281	  \(M|_{U_i}\) corresponds (via the equivalence between
282	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\Spec A_i}\)-modules and \(A_i\)-modules) to an
283	  \(A_i\)-module \(M_i\), and similarly
284	  \(N|_{U_i}\) corresponds to an \(A_i\)-module \(N_i\). Define
285	  \((M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\) as the sheafification of the presheaf
286	  \(U_i \mapsto M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i\). On overlaps
287	  \(U_i \cap U_j = \Spec A_{ij}\) the resulting modules agree via the
288	  canonical compatibility
289	  \((M_i \otimes_{A_i} N_i) \otimes_{A_i} A_{ij}
290	     \;\cong\;
291	     (M_j \otimes_{A_j} N_j) \otimes_{A_j} A_{ij}\),
292	  so the data \(\{(M \otimes_X N)|_{U_i}\}\) glue to a single object
293	  \(M \otimes_X N\) in \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\). The result is
294	  canonically independent of the affine cover chosen.
295	
296	  Equivalently: the underlying presheaf of \(M \otimes_X N\) is the
297	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product
298	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) of the underlying
299	  presheaves of \(M\) and \(N\), composed with the sheafification
300	  functor on the small Zariski site of \(X\). The substrate operation
301	  \(\otimes_X\) is, by construction, a \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
302	  object reflecting the relative tensor product of
303	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described in Stacks tag 03DM.
304	\end{definition}
305	
306	\begin{lemma}
307	
308	\leanok
309	[Functoriality of $\otimes_X$]
310	  \label{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}
311	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_functoriality}
312	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
313	  Let \(X\) be a scheme. The assignment
314	  \((M, N) \mapsto M \otimes_X N\) of
315	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} extends to a bifunctor
316	  \[
317	    \otimes_X \;\;\colon\;\; \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\times\;
318	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X \;\;\longrightarrow\;\;
319	                          \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X
320	  \]
321	  natural in both arguments: a pair of morphisms
322	  \(f : M \to M'\) and \(g : N \to N'\) determines a morphism
323	  \(f \otimes g : M \otimes_X N \to M' \otimes_X N'\) compatible with
324	  identities and composition. This bifunctorial action on morphisms is
325	  the only datum the present lemma provides. The further coherence data
326	  of a monoidal structure --- the unitors
327	  \[
328	    \lambda_M \;\colon\; \mathcal{O}_X \otimes_X M \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
329	    \qquad
330	    \rho_M \;\colon\; M \otimes_X \mathcal{O}_X \;\xrightarrow{\sim}\; M,
331	  \]
332	  the associator
333	  \(\alpha_{M, N, P} : (M \otimes_X N) \otimes_X P \xrightarrow{\sim}
334	   M \otimes_X (N \otimes_X P)\), and the braiding
335	  \(\beta_{M, N} : M \otimes_X N \xrightarrow{\sim} N \otimes_X M\) ---
336	  are \emph{not} outputs of this lemma; they are off the critical path,
337	  recorded in \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
338	\end{lemma}
339	
340	\begin{proof}
341	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
342	  Pick an affine open cover \(\{U_i = \Spec A_i\}\) of \(X\). On each
343	  affine, the assignment is the standard tensor product of modules over a
344	  commutative ring \(A_i\), which is bifunctorial. The bifunctorial action
345	  on morphisms glues across the cover because the underlying gluing data of
346	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} respect the canonical morphisms on
347	  overlaps; equivalently, the morphism action is inherited from
348	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj}\) (Mathlib module
349	  \texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.PresheafOfModules}) under
350	  sheafification. The unitor/associator/braiding coherence data are
351	  \emph{not} constructed here; they are off the critical path
352	  (\cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}).
353	\end{proof}
354	
355	\begin{remark}
356	[The full monoidal category is cheap via route~(e); the group law consumes only propositions]
357	  \label{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}
358	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj, lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality,
359	        lem:whisker_of_W}
360	  One could ask for the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) of
361	  \cref{lem:scheme_modules_tensorobj_functoriality}, together with
362	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\), \(\alpha\), \(\lambda\), \(\rho\) and \(\beta\), to
363	  assemble into a full symmetric \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure on
364	  \emph{all} of \(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\), discharging the pentagon,
365	  triangle and hexagon coherence identities. Under route~(e) this is
366	  \emph{cheap}, not expensive: it is exactly the output of
367	  \(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) applied to the
368	  already-monoidal presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\)
369	  and the sheafification localizer \(J.W\), once the single instance
370	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}) is supplied. All the
371	  coherence is then derived by the API.
372	
373	  Crucially, \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) needs \emph{no} closed structure. Its
374	  \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\) field \(W\,g \Rightarrow W(F \triangleleft g)\) for an
375	  \emph{arbitrary} module \(F\) is the flatness-free stalkwise statement
376	  \cref{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}: a \(J.W\)-morphism \(g\) is a
377	  stalkwise isomorphism, so \((F \triangleleft g)_x = \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes
378	  g_x\) is again an isomorphism for any \(F\) (tensoring with an isomorphism
379	  preserves isomorphisms). This is the same technique Mathlib uses for
380	  fixed-base presheaves in
381	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\), ported to
382	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). It uses neither
383	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R)\) nor the
384	  sectionwise-flatness hypothesis of \cref{lem:flat_whisker_localizer} (which is
385	  false over a non-affine open and is therefore off the critical path); the
386	  earlier reading that arbitrary-\(F\) whisker stability requires
387	  \(\mathtt{MonoidalClosed}\) was the \emph{wrong altitude}.
388	
389	  Independently of how the monoidal structure is built, the relative Picard
390	  group law of \cref{lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid} is a structure on
391	  \emph{isomorphism classes} of line bundles, and every group axiom there is a
392	  \emph{proposition} of the form \(\mathrm{Nonempty}(\cdots \cong \cdots)\).
393	  Such a proposition is insensitive to which isomorphism witnesses it, so the
394	  coherence data of the monoidal category --- pentagon, triangle, hexagon, and
395	  a fortiori any monoidal-closed structure --- is never \emph{consumed} by the
396	  group law. The group law needs only the existence of the three coherence
397	  isomorphisms \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,%
398	  lem:tensorobj_comm_iso}, all now supplied by the route~(e) monoidal structure;
399	  none of them is built from the off-path restriction-compatibility isomorphism
400	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}. This mirrors Mathlib's own Picard group
401	  \(\mathtt{CommRing.Pic}\,R\) (\texttt{Mathlib.RingTheory.PicardGroup}),
402	  whose \(\mathtt{CommGroup}\) is transported from
403	  \(\mathrm{Units}\bigl(\mathrm{Skeleton}(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\,R)\bigr)\): there
404	  over a fixed ring, here over the sheafification localizer via
405	  \(\mathtt{LocalizedMonoidal}\).
406	\end{remark}
407	
408	\section{The lift through \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\)}
409	\label{sec:tensorobj_onproduct_lift}
410	
411	The consumer in chapter \cref{chap:Picard_RelPicFunctor} works not with
412	\(\Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}(C \times_k T)\) directly but with the subtype
413	\(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\,\pi_C\,\pi_T\) of locally-trivial
414	modules on the fiber product \(C \times_k T\), introduced in
415	\cref{chap:Picard_LineBundlePullback}. This section records the
416	\emph{existence-of-isomorphism facts on line bundles} (associativity, unit,
417	commutativity and inverse) that the relative Picard group law consumes ---
418	under route~(e) these are read off the \(\MonoidalCategory\) structure of
419	\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal} --- together with the closure of \(\otimes\) on the
420	locally-trivial \(\texttt{LineBundle.OnProduct}\) subtype
421	(\cref{lem:tensorobj_lift_onproduct}) and the interaction with the pullback
422	functor \(\pi_T^*\). The open-immersion restriction-compatibility isomorphism
423	\cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso} is retained as an off-path supplement, not as a
424	group-law ingredient; cf.\ \cref{rem:scheme_modules_monoidal_off_path}.
425	
426	\begin{lemma}
427	
428	\leanok
429	[Sectionwise lax-monoidal structure on \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\)]
430	  \label{lem:restrictscalars_laxmonoidal}
431	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal}
432	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
433	  % NOTE: this CommRingCat-level lax-monoidal instance
434	  % (`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalarsLaxMonoidal`, with helpers
435	  % `restrictScalarsLaxε`, `restrictScalarsLaxμ`) is a self-contained
436	  % supplement. It is NOT used by `lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso`, whose proof
437	  % (open-immersion sectionwise base change) identifies restriction along an
438	  % open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
439	  % needs no lax-monoidal pushforward. Retained for potential reuse; off the
440	  % critical path for the tensor group law.
441	  Let \(\varphi : R \to S\) be a morphism of presheaves of \emph{commutative}
442	  rings on a site (so that both \(R\) and \(S\) factor through
443	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) and the category of presheaves of modules over each
444	  is monoidal). Then the presheaf-of-modules restriction-of-scalars functor
445	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) is lax monoidal.
446	  Consequently, composing with the already-monoidal Mathlib functor
447	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) via
448	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\), the full pushforward
449	  \[
450	    \mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi
451	      \;\;:=\;\;
452	    \mathtt{pushforward}_0\,F\,R \;\circ\; \mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi
453	  \]
454	  is lax monoidal. This lemma is a self-contained
455	  \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-level lax-monoidal supplement: it stands on its own
456	  and is retained for potential reuse, but it is \emph{off the critical path}
457	  for the tensor group law. In particular it is \emph{not} an ingredient of
458	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}, whose proof identifies restriction along an
459	  open immersion with base change along a structure-sheaf isomorphism and
460	  consequently needs no lax-monoidal structure on the pushforward.
461	\end{lemma}
462	
463	\begin{proof}
464	  This is a \emph{sectionwise} lift of the existing fact that, for a ring map
465	  \(f : R \to S\), the module restriction-of-scalars functor
466	  \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,f\) is lax monoidal
467	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Monoidal.Adjunction}, where it is
468	  obtained as the \(\mathtt{rightAdjointLaxMonoidal}\) of the
469	  extension/restriction adjunction \(\mathtt{extendRestrictScalarsAdj}\) ---
470	  i.e.\ Mathlib already uses the same mate idiom one level down). The presheaf
471	  monoidal structure is computed sectionwise,
472	  \((M_1 \otimes M_2).\mathrm{obj}\,X = M_1.\mathrm{obj}\,X \otimes M_2.\mathrm{obj}\,X\),
473	  and the presheaf functor \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\,\varphi\) acts
474	  sectionwise as \(\mathtt{ModuleCat.restrictScalars}\,(\varphi.\mathrm{app}\,X)\).
475	  Hence the lax structure maps \(\mu\) (laxity) and \(\varepsilon\) (unit)
476	  assemble from the per-section \(\mathtt{ModuleCat}\) ones, the required
477	  naturality squares being the naturality of the sectionwise maps against the
478	  presheaf restriction morphisms. Packaging these as a
479	  \(\mathtt{Functor.CoreLaxMonoidal}\) yields the lax-monoidal structure; no
480	  new diagram chase is needed beyond transporting the sectionwise coherence.
481	  The composite \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\) is then lax monoidal by
482	  \(\mathtt{Functor.LaxMonoidal.comp}\) together with the monoidality of
483	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}_0\mathtt{OfCommRingCat}\) (Mathlib), modulo a defeq
484	  transport across the definitional unfolding of \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\varphi\).
485	
486	  The commutativity hypothesis is essential: the presheaf-of-modules monoidal
487	  category requires \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-valued presheaves of rings, so the
488	  instance must be stated over \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\)-factored \(R, S\). In
489	  the project this holds: the relevant \(\varphi\) is
490	  \((\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\), which is
491	  structure-sheaf-valued and hence commutative-ring-valued.
492	\end{proof}
493	
494	\begin{lemma}
495	
496	\leanok
497	[Tensor product commutes with restriction along an open immersion]
498	  \label{lem:tensorobj_restrict_iso}
499	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_restrict_iso}
500	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
501	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, let \(M, N \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be
502	  \emph{arbitrary} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules, and let
503	  \(f : U \hookrightarrow X\) be an open immersion, with \((-)|_f\) the
504	  restriction of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along \(f\). The canonical comparison
505	  morphism
506	  \[
507	    (M \otimes_X N)\big|_f
508	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\;}\;
509	    M\big|_f \;\otimes_U\; N\big|_f
510	  \]
511	  is an isomorphism. No local-freeness, flatness, or line-bundle hypothesis on
512	  \(M, N\) is required: the statement holds for all \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules.
513	  The reason is structural to open immersions --- restriction along \(f\) is
514	  base change along the structure-sheaf \emph{isomorphism} induced by \(f\),
515	  not along a general ring map --- and base change along a ring isomorphism is
516	  trivially invertible and commutes with tensor products.
517	\end{lemma}
518	
519	\begin{proof}
520	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
521	  Write \(\varphi\) for the morphism of structure-sheaf-valued presheaves of
522	  rings induced by the open immersion \(f\) (concretely
523	  \(\varphi = (\mathtt{Scheme.Hom.toRingCatSheafHom}\,f).\mathrm{hom}\)), so that
524	  restriction along \(f\) is computed by the inverse-image (pullback) functor on
525	  presheaves of modules. The proof exploits the defining feature of an open
526	  immersion: the comparison of structure-sheaf rings over the image is an
527	  \emph{isomorphism}, not merely a ring map. For an open \(V \subseteq U\) the
528	  section ring \(\Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr)\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) over the image
529	  of \(V\) is identified with \(\Gamma(U, V)\) by the canonical \emph{ring
530	  isomorphism}
531	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\,V : \Gamma\bigl(X, f(V)\bigr) \xrightarrow{\sim}
532	  \Gamma(U, V)\)
533	  (the \(\mathtt{CommRingCat}\) isomorphism
534	  \(\mathtt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Hom.appIso}\)). Restriction along \(f\) is
535	  therefore extension of scalars along
536	  this ring isomorphism, and the proof needs no flatness anywhere.
537	
538	  \emph{Step 1 (reduce restriction to the abstract pullback).} The restriction
539	  \((-)|_f\) of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules along the open immersion \(f\) is
540	  naturally isomorphic to the abstract presheaf-of-modules pullback
541	  \((\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(-)\), via the
542	  present comparison \(\mathtt{Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback}\). It
543	  therefore suffices to produce the comparison and prove it an isomorphism for
544	  the abstract pullback functor.
545	
546	  \emph{Step 2 (move the pullback inside the sheafification).} The substrate
547	  \(\otimes_X\) is sheafification of the presheaf-of-modules tensor product
548	  (\cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}). The pullback commutes with
549	  sheafification: there is a natural isomorphism
550	  \(\mathtt{sheafification} \circ \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi
551	   \;\cong\;
552	   \mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi \circ \mathtt{sheafification}\)
553	  (the present \(\mathtt{SheafOfModules.sheafificationCompPullback}\)). Hence the
554	  whole question descends to the presheaf level, where it is the base-change
555	  comparison
556	  \[
557	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}(A \otimes B)
558	      \;\longrightarrow\;
559	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,A
560	      \;\otimes\;
561	    (\mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi).\mathrm{obj}\,B
562	  \]
563	  for the underlying presheaves of modules \(A, B\) of \(M\) and \(N\).
564	
565	  \emph{Step 3 (off the critical path).} Completing this comparison at the
566	  presheaf level requires a sectionwise description of the abstract functor
567	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.pullback}\,\varphi\), which is defined as the left
568	  adjoint of pushforward and admits no sectionwise formula in Mathlib at the
569	  pinned commit. The genuine residual decomposes into two Mathlib-absent
570	  presheaf-level ingredients --- a presheaf-level identification
571	  \(\mathtt{pushforward}\,\beta \cong \mathtt{pullback}\,\varphi\) via
572	  \(\mathtt{Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq}\) (needing a presheaf-level pushforward
573	  adjunction, only the sheaf version of which exists), and a strong-monoidal
574	  upgrade of \(\mathtt{restrictScalars}\) along the ring isomorphism
575	  \(f.\mathtt{appIso}\). This lemma is therefore \emph{not} on the critical path
576	  for the relative Picard group law: the group law is built from
577	  \(\otimes\)-invertibility (\cref{def:scheme_modules_isinvertible}) and the
578	  coherence isomorphisms
579	  \cref{lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso,lem:tensorobj_unit_iso,lem:tensorobj_comm_iso},
580	  none of which consume restriction-compatibility. The statement is retained
581	  only as an optional supplement; its full proof is deferred.
582	\end{proof}

584	\begin{lemma}
585	
586	\leanok
587	[Tensor product of locally-trivial modules is locally trivial]
588	  \label{lem:tensorobj_preserves_locally_trivial}
589	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial}
590	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
591	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, and let
592	  \(L, L' \in \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\) be locally-trivial modules of
593	  rank one (i.e.\ line bundles). The tensor product \(L \otimes_X L'\) of
594	  \cref{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj} is again locally trivial of rank
595	  one. Consequently the bifunctor \(\otimes_X\) restricts to a bifunctor
596	  on the full subcategory \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X \subset \Scheme.\mathtt{Modules}\,X\)
597	  of locally-trivial rank-one modules.
598	\end{lemma}
599	
600	\begin{proof}
601	  Pick a common affine open cover \(\{U_i\}\) of \(X\) on which both
602	  \(L|_{U_i}\) and \(L'|_{U_i}\) are free of rank one. On each \(U_i\)
603	  there are trivialisations \(L|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\) and
604	  \(L'|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). The tensor product of two free
605	  rank-one modules over a commutative ring is again free of rank one;
606	  hence \((L \otimes_X L')|_{U_i} \cong \mathcal{O}_{U_i}\). So
607	  \(L \otimes_X L'\) is locally trivial of rank one, i.e.\ an object of
608	  \(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X\).
609	\end{proof}
610	
611	\begin{lemma}
612	
613	[Flat whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
614	  \label{lem:flat_whisker_localizer}
615	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerLeft_of_flat, PresheafOfModules.W_whiskerRight_of_flat}
616	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
617	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
618	  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the class of morphisms of presheaves of
619	  \(R\)-modules that are inverted by sheafification --- equivalently, the
620	  \emph{locally bijective} morphisms, i.e.\ those that are both locally injective
621	  and locally surjective for \(J\) (the characterisation
622	  \(\mathtt{WEqualsLocallyBijective}\)). Let \(F\) be a presheaf of \(R\)-modules
623	  that is \emph{flat} (sectionwise: \(F(U)\) is a flat \(R(U)\)-module for every
624	  object \(U\) of the site). Then for every morphism \(g\) in \(J.W\) the
625	  left-whiskered morphism
626	  \[
627	    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
628	  \]
629	  (the action of \(F \otimes^{p} -\) on \(g\), with \(\otimes^{p}\) the
630	  presheaf-of-modules tensor product) again lies in \(J.W\). By the symmetry of
631	  the presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, the right-whiskered morphism
632	  \(g \triangleright F\) likewise lies in \(J.W\) when \(F\) is flat.
633	
634	  Both halves --- the left-whiskering statement and its braiding-conjugate
635	  right-whiskering statement --- are established. They are valid standalone
636	  results about flat whiskering of sheafification-localizer morphisms. They are,
637	  however, \emph{off the critical path}: the live whiskering-stability data for
638	  \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (route~(e)) are the flatness-free variants
639	  \(\mathtt{W\_whiskerLeft\_of\_W}\) / \(\mathtt{W\_whiskerRight\_of\_W}\)
640	  (\cref{lem:whisker_of_W}), whose stalkwise proof holds for an \emph{arbitrary}
641	  whiskering object \(F\) and never invokes the sectionwise-flatness hypothesis
642	  of the present lemma. The sectionwise flatness condition --- \(F(U)\) flat over
643	  \(R(U)\) for \emph{every} object \(U\) of the site, including non-affine opens
644	  --- is not supplied by \(\otimes\)-invertibility or local triviality, which are
645	  affine-local properties; the present (flat) lemma is therefore retained as an
646	  independent, closed result, superseded on the critical path by the
647	  \(\mathtt{\_of\_W}\) variants of \cref{lem:whisker_of_W}.
648	\end{lemma}
649	
650	\begin{proof}
651	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
652	  By the locally-bijective characterisation of the sheafification localizer
653	  (\(J.W\) is exactly the class of morphisms that are \(J\)-locally injective and
654	  \(J\)-locally surjective), it suffices to show that \(F \triangleleft g\) is
655	  both locally injective and locally surjective whenever \(g\) is.
656	
657	  The presheaf-of-modules tensor product is computed sectionwise,
658	  \((F \otimes^{p} G)(U) = F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} G(U)\), so for
659	  \(g : A \to B\) the whiskered morphism acts on sections over \(U\) as the
660	  \(R(U)\)-linear map
661	  \[
662	    \mathrm{id}_{F(U)} \otimes g(U) \;\colon\;
663	      F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} A(U) \;\longrightarrow\; F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} B(U).
664	  \]
665	
666	  \emph{Local surjectivity} is automatic and needs no hypothesis on \(F\):
667	  tensoring is right exact, so applying \(F \otimes^{p} -\) carries the cokernel
668	  of \(g\) to the cokernel of \(F \triangleleft g\); since \(g\) is locally
669	  surjective its cokernel is locally zero, hence so is the cokernel of
670	  \(F \triangleleft g\), and \(F \triangleleft g\) is locally surjective.
671	
672	  \emph{Local injectivity} is where flatness enters. Because each \(F(U)\) is a
673	  flat \(R(U)\)-module, the functor \(F(U) \otimes_{R(U)} -\) preserves injective
674	  linear maps (Mathlib
675	  \(\mathtt{Module.Flat.lTensor\_preserves\_injective\_linearMap}\)). Thus
676	  whenever \(g(U)\) is injective on a covering sieve, so is
677	  \((\mathrm{id}_{F(U)} \otimes g(U))\); the local injectivity of \(g\) is
678	  therefore transported sectionwise to \(F \triangleleft g\).
679	
680	  Locally injective and locally surjective together give
681	  \(F \triangleleft g \in J.W\). The right-whiskered statement
682	  \(g \triangleright F\) follows by conjugating \(F \triangleleft g\) with the
683	  braiding of the symmetric presheaf-of-modules monoidal structure, which is an
684	  isomorphism and hence preserves membership in \(J.W\); the flatness of \(F\) is
685	  the only input, and it is symmetric in the two tensor factors.
686	\end{proof}
687	
688	\begin{lemma}
689	
690	\leanok
691	[Sheafification inverts a localizer morphism of presheaves of modules]
692	  \label{lem:isiso_sheafification_map_of_W}
693	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.isIso_sheafification_map_of_W}
694	  Let \(R_0\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a
695	  Grothendieck topology \(J\), let \(R_{sh}\) be its sheafification, and let
696	  \(\alpha : R_0 \to R_{sh}.\mathrm{obj}\) be the (locally bijective) comparison
697	  map of presheaves of rings. Let \(f : A \to B\) be a morphism of presheaves of
698	  \(R_0\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
699	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0).\mathrm{map}\,f\) lies in the sheafification
700	  localizer class \(J.W\) --- equivalently, is locally bijective for \(J\). Then
701	  the presheaf-of-modules sheafification functor
702	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.sheafification}\,\alpha\) sends \(f\) to an
703	  isomorphism:
704	  \[
705	    (\mathtt{sheafification}\,\alpha).\mathrm{map}\,f
706	    \quad\text{is an isomorphism.}
707	  \]
708	  This is a closed go/no-go bridge --- it converts ``\,a morphism lies in
709	  \(J.W\)\,'' into ``\,its sheafification is invertible\,''. Under route~(e) the
710	  associator is the API-derived one (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}), so this bridge is
711	  retained as a supplement rather than as the associator's load-bearing step.
712	\end{lemma}
713	
714	\begin{proof}
715	  This is a one-morphism reading of the structural identity that the
716	  sheafification of presheaves of \(R_0\)-modules \emph{is} the localization of
717	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,R_0\) at the class
718	  \(J.W.\mathtt{inverseImage}\,(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0)\) of morphisms whose
719	  underlying map of presheaves of abelian groups is locally bijective. Concretely,
720	  the underlying \(\mathtt{AddCommGrp}\)-valued sheafification inverts exactly the
721	  locally bijective morphisms, and Mathlib's
722	  \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules.inverseImage\_W\_toPresheaf\_eq\_inverseImage\_%
723	  isomorphisms}\) identifies the preimage of \(J.W\) under
724	  \(\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0\) with the preimage of the class of isomorphisms under
725	  the module-level sheafification functor. Reading that equality of morphism
726	  classes at the single morphism \(f\): the hypothesis
727	  \(J.W\,\bigl((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R_0).\mathrm{map}\,f\bigr)\) places \(f\) in
728	  that preimage, hence \((\mathtt{sheafification}\,\alpha).\mathrm{map}\,f\) is an
729	  isomorphism. No new construction is required beyond instantiating the existing
730	  Mathlib identity at one morphism; this lemma is a closed thin wrapper, retained
731	  as a supplement under route~(e).
732	\end{proof}

734	\section{Route~(e): the \(J.W\)-monoidality instance and the localized monoidal structure}
735	\label{sec:tensorobj_route_e}
736	
737	The monoidal structure on the substrate is realized by \emph{instantiating}
738	Mathlib's localization-of-monoidal-categories API
739	(\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}): apply
740	\(\mathtt{Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal}\) to the already-monoidal
741	presheaf category \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal{O}_X\) and the
742	sheafification localizer \(J.W\). The single new input the API requires is the
743	instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\), whose two fields are the
744	whiskering-stability lemmas below. Their load-bearing local-injectivity half is
745	the sole remaining open obligation of the entire group-law engine.
746	
747	\begin{lemma}
748	[Local injectivity of a whiskered localizer morphism (stalkwise, flatness-free)]
749	  \label{lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W}
750	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W}
751	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
752	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of commutative rings on a site carrying a Grothendieck
753	  topology \(J\), and let \(J.W\) be the sheafification localizer (the locally
754	  bijective morphisms). Let \(F\) be an \emph{arbitrary} presheaf of
755	  \(R\)-modules, and let \(g : M \to N\) be a morphism of presheaves of
756	  \(R\)-modules whose underlying morphism of presheaves of abelian groups
757	  \((\mathtt{toPresheaf}\,R).\mathrm{map}\,g\) lies in \(J.W\). Then the
758	  left-whiskered morphism
759	  \[
760	    F \triangleleft g \;\;=\;\; \mathrm{id}_F \otimes g
761	  \]
762	  is \emph{\(J\)-locally injective}. No flatness and no local-triviality
763	  hypothesis on \(F\) is required.
764	
765	  This is the \(\mathtt{whiskerLeft}\)-field content (the injectivity half) of the
766	  instance \(J.W.\mathtt{IsMonoidal}\) (\cref{lem:jw_ismonoidal}), and it is
767	  \emph{the sole remaining open obligation for the whole group-law engine}: every
768	  other ingredient of route~(e) is either supplied by Mathlib or closed
769	  project-side.
770	\end{lemma}
771	
772	\begin{proof}
773	  \uses{def:scheme_modules_tensorobj}
774	  The argument is the stalkwise one used by Mathlib for fixed-base presheaves in
775	  \(\mathtt{CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.IsMonoidalW}\)
776	  (\cref{sec:tensorobj_api_survey}), ported to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\). A
777	  morphism in \(J.W\) is, on the topological site of \(X\), a \emph{stalkwise
778	  isomorphism}: at each point \(x\) the induced map on stalks \(g_x\) is an
779	  isomorphism. The presheaf-of-modules tensor is sectionwise, so it commutes with
780	  the stalk (a filtered colimit), giving
781	  \[
782	    (F \triangleleft g)_x
783	      \;=\; \mathrm{id}_{F_x} \otimes_{R_x} g_x
784	      \;\colon\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} M_x \;\longrightarrow\; F_x \otimes_{R_x} N_x .
785	  \]
786	  Tensoring an isomorphism \(g_x\) by the identity \(\mathrm{id}_{F_x}\) yields an
787	  isomorphism --- no flatness is needed, since \(g_x\) is invertible, not merely
788	  injective. Hence \(F \triangleleft g\) is a stalkwise isomorphism, in particular
789	  locally bijective, in particular locally injective, for \emph{any} \(F\).
790	
791	  The two ingredients this stalkwise computation needs are, at the pinned commit,
792	  absent from Mathlib at the \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) level, and constitute
793	  the genuine residual:
794	  \begin{enumerate}
795	    \item[(d.1)] the stalkwise characterisation of the module-level \(J.W\) on
796	      \(\mathtt{Opens}\,X\) (that \(g \in J.W\) is equivalent to \(g_x\) being an
797	      isomorphism for every \(x\)); and
798	    \item[(d.2)] the commutation \((A \otimes^{p} B)_x \cong A_x \otimes_{R_x}
799	      B_x\) of the stalk (a filtered colimit) with the relative
800	      module-presheaf tensor (using that \(\mathtt{tensorLeft}\) /
801	      \(\mathtt{tensorRight}\) preserve filtered colimits over a module category).
802	  \end{enumerate}
803	  Mathlib supplies the analogues for presheaves valued in a fixed monoidal
804	  concrete category, but not for modules over a varying ring; porting (d.1)+(d.2)
805	  to \(\mathtt{PresheafOfModules}\) is the remaining work.
806	\end{proof}

809	[Flatness-free whiskering preserves the sheafification localizer]
810	  \label{lem:whisker_of_W}

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2024 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Closed.FunctorCategory.Basic
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Localization.Monoidal.Braided
10	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Equivalence
11	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.SheafHom
12	
13	/-!
14	# Monoidal category structure on categories of sheaves
15	
16	If `A` is a closed braided category with suitable limits,
17	and `J` is a Grothendieck topology with `HasWeakSheafify J A`,
18	then `Sheaf J A` can be equipped with a monoidal category
19	structure. This is not made an instance as in some cases
20	it may conflict with monoidal structure deduced from
21	chosen finite products.
22	
23	## TODO
24	
25	* show that the monoidal category structure on sheaves is closed,
26	  and that the internal hom can be defined in such a way that the
27	  underlying presheaf is the internal hom in the category of presheaves.
28	  Note that a `MonoidalClosed` instance on sheaves can already be obtained
29	  abstractly using the material in `CategoryTheory.Monoidal.Braided.Reflection`.
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	universe v₁ v₂ v₃ u₁ u₂ u₃
36	
37	namespace CategoryTheory
38	
39	variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
40	  {A : Type u₃} [Category.{v₃} A] [MonoidalCategory A]
41	
42	open Opposite Limits MonoidalCategory MonoidalClosed Enriched.FunctorCategory
43	
44	namespace Presheaf
45	
46	variable [MonoidalClosed A]
47	
48	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
49	/-- Relation between `functorEnrichedHom` and `presheafHom`. -/
50	noncomputable def functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv (M : A) (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)
51	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G] (X : C) :
52	    (functorEnrichedHom A F G ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op M)).obj (op X) ≃
53	    (presheafHom (F ⊗ (Functor.const _).obj M) G).obj (op X) where
54	  toFun f :=
55	    { app j := MonoidalClosed.uncurry (f ≫ enrichedHomπ A _ _ (Under.mk j.unop.hom.op))
56	      naturality j j' φ := by
57	        dsimp
58	        rw [tensorHom_id, ← uncurry_natural_right, ← uncurry_pre_app, Category.assoc,
59	          Category.assoc, ← enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerRight,
60	          ← enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerLeft]
61	        congr 2
62	        exact (enrichedHom_condition A (Under.forget (op X) ⋙ F) (Under.forget (op X) ⋙ G)
63	          (i := Under.mk j.unop.hom.op) (j := Under.mk j'.unop.hom.op)
64	            (Under.homMk φ.unop.left.op (Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (by simp)))).symm }
65	  invFun g :=
66	    end_.lift (fun j ↦ MonoidalClosed.curry (g.app (op (Over.mk j.hom.unop)))) (fun j j' φ ↦ by
67	      dsimp
68	      rw [enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerRight,
69	        enrichedOrdinaryCategorySelf_eHomWhiskerLeft,
70	        curry_pre_app, ← curry_natural_right]
71	      congr 1
72	      let α : Over.mk j'.hom.unop ⟶ Over.mk j.hom.unop := Over.homMk φ.right.unop
73	        (Quiver.Hom.op_inj (by simp))
74	      simpa using (g.naturality α.op).symm)
75	  left_inv f := by
76	    dsimp
77	    ext j
78	    dsimp
79	    simp only [curry_uncurry, end_.lift_π]
80	    rfl
81	  right_inv g := by
82	    dsimp
83	    ext j
84	    dsimp
85	    simp only [uncurry_curry, end_.lift_π]
86	    rfl
87	
88	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
89	lemma functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv_naturality
90	    {M : A} {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {X Y : C} (f : X ⟶ Y)
91	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G]
92	    (y : (functorEnrichedHom A F G ⋙ coyoneda.obj (op M)).obj (op Y)) :
93	    functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G X
94	      (y ≫ precompEnrichedHom' _ (Under.map f.op) (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)) =
95	    (presheafHom (F ⊗ (Functor.const Cᵒᵖ).obj M) G).map f.op
96	      (functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G Y y) := by
97	  dsimp
98	  ext ⟨j⟩
99	  simp [functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv, presheafHom]
100	  rfl
101	
102	lemma isSheaf_functorEnrichedHom (F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) (hG : Presheaf.IsSheaf J G)
103	    [HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F G] :
104	    Presheaf.IsSheaf J (functorEnrichedHom A F G) := fun M ↦ by
105	  rw [Presieve.isSheaf_iff_of_nat_equiv
106	    (functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv M F G)
107	    (fun _ _ _ _ ↦ functorEnrichedHomCoyonedaObjEquiv_naturality _ _)]
108	  rw [← isSheaf_iff_isSheaf_of_type]
109	  exact Presheaf.IsSheaf.hom (F ⊗ (Functor.const _).obj M) G hG
110	
111	end Presheaf
112	
113	namespace GrothendieckTopology
114	
115	namespace W
116	
117	variable (J A) in
118	lemma transport_isMonoidal {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] (K : GrothendieckTopology D)
119	    (G : D ⥤ C) [G.IsCoverDense J] [G.Full] [G.IsContinuous K J]
120	    [(G.sheafPushforwardContinuous A K J).EssSurj] [(K.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] :
121	    (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal := by
122	  rw [← J.W_inverseImage_whiskeringLeft K G]
123	  infer_instance
124	
125	variable [MonoidalClosed A]
126	  [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasFunctorEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
127	  [∀ (F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A), HasEnrichedHom A F₁ F₂]
128	
129	open MonoidalClosed.FunctorCategory
130	
131	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
132	lemma whiskerLeft {G₁ G₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {g : G₁ ⟶ G₂} (hg : J.W g) (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :
133	    J.W (F ◁ g) := fun H h ↦ by
134	  have := hg _ (Presheaf.isSheaf_functorEnrichedHom F H h)
135	  rw [← Function.Bijective.of_comp_iff' (f := MonoidalClosed.curry)
136	    ((ihom.adjunction _).homEquiv _ _).bijective]
137	  rw [← Function.Bijective.of_comp_iff (g := MonoidalClosed.curry) _
138	    ((ihom.adjunction _).homEquiv _ _).bijective] at this
139	  convert this using 1
140	  ext α : 1
141	  dsimp
142	  rw [curry_natural_left]
143	
144	lemma whiskerRight [BraidedCategory A]
145	    {F₁ F₂ : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} {f : F₁ ⟶ F₂} (hf : J.W f) (G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) :
146	    J.W (f ▷ G) :=
147	  (J.W.arrow_mk_iso_iff (Arrow.isoMk (β_ F₁ G) (β_ F₂ G))).2 (hf.whiskerLeft G)
148	
149	instance monoidal [BraidedCategory A] : (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal where
150	  whiskerLeft F _ _ _ hg := hg.whiskerLeft F
151	  whiskerRight _ hf G := hf.whiskerRight G
152	
153	end W
154	
155	end GrothendieckTopology
156	
157	namespace Sheaf
158	
159	variable (J A)
160	
161	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` that is obtained
162	by localization of the monoidal category structure on the category
163	of presheaves. -/
164	@[instance_reducible]
165	noncomputable def monoidalCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
166	    MonoidalCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
167	  inferInstanceAs (MonoidalCategory
168	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
169	
170	attribute [local instance] monoidalCategory
171	
172	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` obtained in `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` is
173	braided when `A` is braided. -/
174	@[implicit_reducible]
175	noncomputable def braidedCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
176	    [BraidedCategory A] : BraidedCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
177	  inferInstanceAs (BraidedCategory
178	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
179	
180	/-- The monoidal category structure on `Sheaf J A` obtained in `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` is
181	symmetric when `A` is symmetric. -/
182	@[implicit_reducible]
183	noncomputable def symmetricCategory [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A]
184	    [SymmetricCategory A] :
185	    SymmetricCategory (Sheaf J A) :=
186	  inferInstanceAs (SymmetricCategory
187	    (LocalizedMonoidal (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)))
188	
189	noncomputable instance [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] :
190	    (presheafToSheaf J A).Monoidal :=
191	  inferInstanceAs (Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory
192	    (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)).Monoidal
193	
194	noncomputable instance [(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal] [HasWeakSheafify J A] [BraidedCategory A] :
195	    letI := braidedCategory J A
196	    (presheafToSheaf J A).Braided :=
197	  inferInstanceAs (Localization.Monoidal.toMonoidalCategory
198	    (L := presheafToSheaf J A) (W := J.W) (Iso.refl _)).Braided
199	
200	noncomputable example
Now let me read `Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean` (the points/stalk template route — used because `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R)` is absent), plus the per-point fiber functor file.

1	/-
2	Copyright (c) 2026 Joël Riou. All rights reserved.
3	Released under Apache 2.0 license as described in the file LICENSE.
4	Authors: Joël Riou
5	-/
6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.Conservative
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.Monoidal
10	
11	/-!
12	# Monoidal structure on sheaves using enough points
13	
14	Let `(C, J)` be a site with a conservative family of points.
15	If `A` is a suitable monoidal category, we show that
16	the class of morphisms `J.W : MorphismProperty (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A)`
17	is stable under tensor products, which allows to
18	check the assumptions of `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` in the
19	file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Monoidal.lean`,
20	i.e. this can be used in order to construct the monoidal
21	category structure on `Sheaf J A`.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe w v v' u u'
28	
29	namespace CategoryTheory
30	
31	open Limits GrothendieckTopology MonoidalCategory
32	
33	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] [LocallySmall.{w} C]
34	  {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
35	  {P : ObjectProperty (Point.{w} J)} (hP : P.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints)
36	  (A : Type u') [Category.{v'} A] [MonoidalCategory A]
37	  [HasColimitsOfSize.{w, w} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]
38	  {FC : A → A → Type*} {CC : A → Type w}
39	  [∀ (X Y : A), FunLike (FC X Y) (CC X) (CC Y)]
40	  [ConcreteCategory.{w} A FC]
41	  [HasWeakSheafify J A]
42	  [(forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]
43	  [PreservesFilteredColimitsOfSize.{w, w} (forget A)]
44	  [∀ (X : A), PreservesFilteredColimitsOfSize.{w, w} (tensorLeft X)]
45	  [∀ (X : A), PreservesFilteredColimitsOfSize.{w, w} (tensorRight X)]
46	
47	include hP in
48	lemma ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.isMonoidal_W
49	    [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] :
50	    (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal :=
51	  .mk' _ (fun f g hf hg ↦ by
52	    simp only [hP.W_iff (A := A)] at hf hg ⊢
53	    intro Φ
54	    rw [Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor]
55	    infer_instance)
56	
57	instance [J.HasSheafCompose (forget A)] [HasEnoughPoints.{w} J] :
58	    (J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal := by
59	  obtain ⟨P, _, hP⟩ := HasEnoughPoints.exists_objectProperty J
60	  exact hP.isMonoidal_W A
61	
62	end CategoryTheory
63

The two Mathlib `IsMonoidal` instances both require a **fixed** monoidal concrete category `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` — neither applies to `PresheafOfModules` (varying ring). The points route (`IsMonoidalW`) needs `HasEnoughPoints`. Crucially, the current lemma signature is stated for a **general** site `C` with no points — a stalk argument can't work at that generality. Let me check protected status and whether the topological site has enough points.

input.command:
cd /home/archon/FormalizationProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge
cat > /tmp/informal_q.md <<'EOF'
In Lean4/Mathlib (category theory of sheaves), I must prove:

Let C be a small site with Grothendieck topology J. Let R : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat be a
presheaf of commutative rings, and PresheafOfModules R the category of presheaves of
R-modules (sectionwise tensor: (M ⊗_R N)(U) = M(U) ⊗_{R(U)} N(U); this is Mathlib's
PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory). Let J.W be the sheafification localizer = the
class of morphisms whose underlying presheaf-of-abelian-groups map is "locally
bijective" (locally injective + locally surjective for J).

GOAL: For an ARBITRARY F : PresheafOfModules R and a morphism g : M ⟶ N of
PresheafOfModules whose underlying Ab-map lies in J.W, show the left-whiskered
F ◁ g (= id_F ⊗ g) is J-locally injective. (No flatness, no local-triviality on F.)

Mathlib already proves the analogous "(J.W).IsMonoidal" for presheaves valued in a
FIXED monoidal concrete category A (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) two ways:
 (i) Sites/Monoidal.lean: via MonoidalClosed A + internal-hom adjunction (NO points);
 (ii) Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean: via a conservative family of points, using
      hP.W_iff (W f ↔ ∀ fiber functor Φ, IsIso (Φ.map f)) then Functor.Monoidal.map_tensor
      then "id ⊗ iso = iso".
Neither applies directly because PresheafOfModules R (modules over a VARYING ring) is
not of the form Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A for fixed monoidal A, and there is no MonoidalClosed
(PresheafOfModules R) in Mathlib.

The site I ultimately need this for is Opens X for a scheme X (which HAS enough points).

Questions:
1. What is the cleanest mathematical route to this goal that minimizes new
   infrastructure? Compare: (a) building a module-stalk fiber functor
   PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat (R_x) plus stalk-commutes-with-tensor, vs
   (b) any way to reduce to the fixed-base Ab/Type machinery, vs (c) anything else.
2. For route (a), what are the minimal lemmas needed and can stalk-commutes-with-

6	module

8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Sites.Point.Conservative
9	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sheaves.Sheaf
10	public import Mathlib.Topology.Sets.Opens
11	
12	/-!
13	# The standard conservative family of points for the site attached to a topological space
14	
15	If `X` is a topological space, any `x : X` defines a point of the site
16	attached to `X`.
17	
18	## TODO
19	
20	* Redefine the stalks functors in `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Stalks.lean`
21	  using `GrothendieckTopology.Point.presheafFiber`.
22	
23	-/
24	
25	@[expose] public section
26	
27	universe u
28	
29	namespace Opens
30	
31	open CategoryTheory GrothendieckTopology TopologicalSpace
32	
33	variable {X : Type u} [TopologicalSpace X] (x : X)
34	
35	/-- Given a topological space `X` and `x : X`, this is the point of the site
36	`(Opens X, Opens.grothendieckTopology X)` corresponding to `x`. -/
37	def pointGrothendieckTopology : Point.{u} (grothendieckTopology X) where
38	  fiber.obj U := ULift.{u} (PLift (x ∈ U))
39	  fiber.map f := TypeCat.ofHom fun h ↦ ⟨⟨leOfHom f h.down.down⟩⟩
40	  isCofiltered :=
41	    { nonempty := ⟨⊤, ⟨⟨by simp⟩⟩⟩
42	      cone_objs := by
43	        rintro ⟨U, ⟨⟨hU⟩⟩⟩ ⟨V, ⟨⟨hV⟩⟩⟩
44	        exact ⟨⟨U ⊓ V, ⟨⟨⟨hU, hV⟩⟩⟩⟩, ⟨homOfLE (by simp), rfl⟩,
45	          ⟨homOfLE (by simp), rfl⟩, ⟨⟩⟩
46	      cone_maps _ _ _ _ := ⟨_, 𝟙 _, rfl⟩ }
47	  initiallySmall := initiallySmall_of_essentiallySmall _
48	  jointly_surjective := by
49	    rintro U R hR ⟨⟨hU⟩⟩
50	    obtain ⟨V, f, hf, hV⟩ := hR x hU
51	    exact ⟨_, _, hf, ⟨⟨hV⟩⟩, rfl⟩
52	
53	variable (X) in
54	/-- When `X` is a topological space, this is the obvious conservative family of
55	points on the site `(Opens X, Opens.grothendieckTopology X)`. -/
56	def pointsGrothendieckTopology : ObjectProperty (Point.{u} (grothendieckTopology X)) :=
57	  ObjectProperty.ofObj pointGrothendieckTopology
58	  deriving ObjectProperty.Small.{u}
59	
60	variable (X) in
61	lemma isConservativeFamilyOfPoints_pointsGrothendieckTopology :
62	    (pointsGrothendieckTopology X).IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints :=
63	  .mk' (fun U S hS x hx ↦ by
64	    obtain ⟨V, f, hf, ⟨⟨hV⟩⟩, _⟩ := hS ⟨_, ⟨x⟩⟩ ⟨⟨hx⟩⟩
65	    exact ⟨V, f, hf, hV⟩)
66	
67	instance : HasEnoughPoints.{u} (grothendieckTopology X) where
68	  exists_objectProperty :=
69	    ⟨_, inferInstance, isConservativeFamilyOfPoints_pointsGrothendieckTopology X⟩
70	
71	instance (U : Opens X) (Φ : Point.{u} (grothendieckTopology X)) :
72	    Subsingleton (Φ.fiber.obj U) :=
73	  Φ.subsingleton_fiber_obj (homOfLE le_top) Limits.isTerminalTop
74	
75	instance : Quiver.IsThin (Point.{u} (grothendieckTopology X)) :=
76	  fun _ _ ↦ ⟨fun _ _ ↦ by ext; subsingleton⟩
77	
78	/-- A point `x` of a topological space `X` specializes to `y` if and only iff
79	there is a (unique) morphism between the corresponding points of the site
80	`(Opens X, grothendieckTopology X)`. -/
81	def pointGrothendieckTopologyHomEquiv {x y : X} :
82	    (pointGrothendieckTopology x ⟶ pointGrothendieckTopology y) ≃ x ⤳ y where
83	  toFun f := specializes_iff_forall_open.2 (fun U h₁ h₂ ↦ (f.hom.app ⟨U, h₁⟩ ⟨⟨h₂⟩⟩).down.down)
84	  invFun s := { hom.app U := TypeCat.ofHom fun hU ↦
85	    ⟨⟨specializes_iff_forall_open.1 s _ U.2 hU.down.down⟩⟩ }
86	  left_inv _ := by subsingleton
87	  right_inv _ := rfl
88	
89	end Opens

14	/-!
15	# Conservative families of points
16	
17	Let `J` be a Grothendieck topology on a category `C`.
18	Let `P : ObjectProperty J.Point` be a family of points. We say that
19	`P` is a conservative family of points if the corresponding
20	fiber functors `Sheaf J (Type w) ⥤ Type w` jointly reflect
21	isomorphisms. Under suitable assumptions on the coefficient
22	category `A`, this implies that the fiber functors
23	`Sheaf J A ⥤ A` corresponding to the points in `P`
24	jointly reflect isomorphisms, epimorphisms and monomorphisms,
25	and they are also jointly faithful.
26	
27	We provide a constructor `ObjectProperty.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints.mk'`
28	which allows to verify that a family of points is conservative
29	using a condition involving covering sieves (SGA 4 IV 6.5 (a)).
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	universe w w' v v' u u'
36	
37	namespace CategoryTheory
38	
39	open Limits Opposite
40	
41	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
42	  (P : ObjectProperty (GrothendieckTopology.Point.{w} J))
43	
44	namespace ObjectProperty
45	
46	/-- Let `P : ObjectProperty J.Point` a family of points of a
47	site `(C, J)`). We say that it is a conservative family of points
48	if the corresponding fiber functors `Sheaf J (Type w) ⥤ Type w`
49	jointly reflect isomorphisms. -/

52	  jointlyReflectIsomorphisms_type :
53	    JointlyReflectIsomorphisms
54	      (fun (Φ : P.FullSubcategory) ↦ Φ.obj.sheafFiber (A := Type w))

59	  (A : Type u') [Category.{v'} A] [LocallySmall.{w} C]

65	  {FC : A → A → Type*} {CC : A → Type w}

67	  [ConcreteCategory.{w} A FC]
68	  [(forget A).ReflectsIsomorphisms]

79	      hP.jointlyReflectIsomorphisms_type.isIso_iff]
80	    exact fun Φ ↦ ((MorphismProperty.isomorphisms _).arrow_mk_iso_iff
81	      (((Functor.mapArrowFunctor _ _).mapIso
82	        (Φ.obj.sheafFiberCompIso (forget A))).app (Arrow.mk f))).2
83	          (inferInstanceAs (IsIso ((forget A).map (Φ.obj.sheafFiber.map f))))

103	    JointlyFaithful

105	  (hP.jointlyReflectIsomorphisms A).jointlyFaithful
106	
107	variable {A} in
108	include hP hJ in
109	lemma W_iff {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : F ⟶ G) [HasWeakSheafify J A]
110	    [HasProducts.{w} A] :
111	    J.W f ↔ ∀ (Φ : P.FullSubcategory), IsIso (Φ.obj.presheafFiber.map f) := by
112	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff, (hP.jointlyReflectIsomorphisms A).isIso_iff]
113	  exact forall_congr'
114	    (fun Φ ↦ (MorphismProperty.isomorphisms A).arrow_mk_iso_iff
115	      (((Functor.mapArrowFunctor _ _).mapIso
116	        (Φ.obj.presheafToSheafCompSheafFiberIso A)).app (Arrow.mk f)))
117

119	include hP in

122	    [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective (Type w)] [HasSheafify J (Type w)]
123	    {X Y : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A} (f : X ⟶ Y)
124	    (hf : ∀ (Φ : P.FullSubcategory),
125	      Function.Surjective (Φ.obj.presheafFiber.map f)) :
126	    Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f := by
127	  simp only [← ofHom_epi_iff_surjective] at hf
128	  rw [Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_whisker_forget,
129	    ← Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_presheafToSheaf_map_iff,
130	    Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi,
131	    (hP.jointlyReflectEpimorphisms (Type w)).epi_iff]
132	  exact fun Φ ↦ ((MorphismProperty.epimorphisms (Type w)).arrow_mk_iso_iff
133	    (((Functor.mapArrowFunctor _ _).mapIso
134	      ((Φ.obj.presheafFiberCompIso (forget A)).symm ≪≫
135	        Functor.isoWhiskerLeft _ (Φ.obj.presheafToSheafCompSheafFiberIso (Type w)).symm)).app
136	          (Arrow.mk f))).1 (hf Φ)

138	end
139	
140	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
141	lemma jointly_reflect_ofArrows_mem
142	    [HasSheafify J (Type w)] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective (Type w)]
143	    (hP : P.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints)
144	    {X : C} {ι : Type*} [Small.{w} ι] {U : ι → C} (f : ∀ i, U i ⟶ X) :
145	    Sieve.ofArrows _ f ∈ J X ↔

164	    [HasSheafify J (Type w)] [J.WEqualsLocallyBijective (Type w)]
165	    (hP : P.IsConservativeFamilyOfPoints) [ObjectProperty.Small.{w} P]
166	    {X : C} {ι : Type*} {U : ι → C} (f : ∀ i, U i ⟶ X) :
167	    Sieve.ofArrows _ f ∈ J X ↔
168	      ∀ (Φ : P.FullSubcategory) (x : Φ.obj.fiber.obj X),
169	        ∃ (i : ι) (y : Φ.obj.fiber.obj (U i)), Φ.obj.fiber.map (f i) y = x := by
170	  refine ⟨fun hf Φ x ↦ ?_, fun hf ↦ ?_⟩
171	  · obtain ⟨Z, _, ⟨_, p, _, ⟨i⟩, rfl⟩, z, rfl⟩ := Φ.obj.jointly_surjective _ hf x
172	    exact ⟨i, Φ.obj.fiber.map p z, by simp⟩
173	  · let ι' : Type _ := Σ (Φ : P.FullSubcategory), Φ.obj.fiber.obj X
174	    choose i y hy using fun (j : ι') ↦ hf j.1 j.2
175	    refine J.superset_covering (S := Sieve.ofArrows _ (fun i' ↦ f (i i'))) ?_ ?_

177	      exact fun _ ↦ Sieve.ofArrows_mk _ _ _
178	    · rw [hP.jointly_reflect_ofArrows_mem]
179	      exact fun Φ x ↦ ⟨_, _, hy ⟨Φ, x⟩⟩

183	    (hP : ∀ ⦃X : C⦄ (S : Sieve X) (_ : ∀ (Φ : P.FullSubcategory) (x : Φ.obj.fiber.obj X),
184	      ∃ (Y : C) (g : Y ⟶ X) (_ : S g) (y : Φ.obj.fiber.obj Y), Φ.obj.fiber.map g y = x),
185	        S ∈ J X)

187	    (hf : ∀ (Φ : P.FullSubcategory), Function.Surjective (Φ.obj.presheafFiber.map f)) :
188	    Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective J f := by

Major finding: Mathlib already provides the **module stalk** `Module (R.stalk x) (stalk M.presheaf x)` + `germ_smul` for `X : TopCat`, `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`. This is the d.1/d.2 foundation — but it's topological-site-specific, whereas the current lemma is over a general site `C`. Let me find the two remaining bridges: (d.1) `J.W ⟺ stalkwise iso` criteria, and (d.2) tensor–filtered-colimit commutation.

Mathlib has the classical TopCat sheaf-stalk iso criteria, plus the module-stalk structure. The full d.1 (site-`W` ⟺ stalkwise iso bridge) + d.2 (tensor–filtered-colimit commutation over a varying ring) is genuinely a multi-iteration infrastructure build. Per mathlib-build mode I should still land a concrete axiom-clean brick. The most self-contained, reusable d.1 ingredient is the **`R.stalk x`-linear stalk map** of a `PresheafOfModules` morphism (using the existing module-stalk structure + `germ_smul`). Let me probe the stalk-map API.

747	commutes with the pullback of presheaves (sectionwise this is the

universe u

noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
    (TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) →ₗ[R.stalk x]
      (TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) where
  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
  map_add' a b := by rw [map_add]
  map_smul' r s := by
    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
    -- represent r and s as germs on a common open
    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
    -- restrict both to W = U ⊓ V
    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
    sorry
end PresheafOfModules

import Mathlib
open CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite

universe u
namespace PresheafOfModules
variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}

noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
  map_smul' r s := by
    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
    -- restrict representatives to the common open W
    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
    rw [hr, hs]
    sorry
end PresheafOfModules

end FlatWhisker

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map
(ROUTE (e), ingredient d.1)

The route-(e) residual `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` is a stalkwise
argument: a `J.W`-morphism `g` is a *stalkwise isomorphism*, so `(F ◁ g)_x =
id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is again an isomorphism for arbitrary `F`. The stalkwise
characterisation it ultimately rests on (ingredient d.1) requires the induced
Ab-stalk map of a morphism `g : M ⟶ N` of presheaves of `R`-modules to be packaged
as an **`R.stalk x`-linear map** between the stalk modules.

Mathlib (`Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`) already supplies, for `X : TopCat`
and `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, the stalk module structure
`Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` together with the germ /
scalar compatibility `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul`; what it does **not** supply is the
linearity of the induced stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)`.
This section provides that packaging (the first concrete ingredient of d.1 toward
`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`). The base ring presheaf is necessarily
`CommRingCat`-valued, matching the project's `X.presheaf` carrier. -/

section StalkLinearMap

open TopologicalSpace TopCat.Presheaf Opposite

variable {X : TopCat.{u}} {R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat.{u}}

/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a morphism of presheaves of modules.**
For `g : M ⟶ N` in `PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)` over a topological space
`X` and a point `x`, the induced Ab-stalk map `(stalkFunctor Ab x).map
((toPresheaf _).map g) : M.presheaf.stalk x ⟶ N.presheaf.stalk x` is `R.stalk x`-linear
for the stalk module structures of `Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Stalk`.
Project-local: Mathlib packages the stalk module structure (`germ_smul`) but not the
linearity of the induced stalk map. This is ingredient (d.1) of the route-(e)
stalkwise argument for `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`. -/
noncomputable def stalkLinearMap
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X) :
    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) →ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) where
  toFun := (ConcreteCategory.hom
    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
  map_add' a b := map_add _ a b
  map_smul' r s := by
    dsimp only [RingHom.id_apply]
    obtain ⟨U, hxU, r₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist R x r
    obtain ⟨V, hxV, s₀, rfl⟩ := TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist M.presheaf x s
    set W : Opens X := U ⊓ V with hW
    have hxW : x ∈ W := ⟨hxU, hxV⟩
    set iWU : W ⟶ U := homOfLE inf_le_left
    set iWV : W ⟶ V := homOfLE inf_le_right
    have hr : (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ U x hxU)) r₀
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (R.germ W x hxW)) ((ConcreteCategory.hom (R.map iWU.op)) r₀) :=
      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply R iWU x hxW r₀).symm
    have hs : (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf V x hxV)) s₀
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW))
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (M.presheaf.map iWV.op)) s₀) :=
      (TopCat.Presheaf.germ_res_apply M.presheaf iWV x hxW s₀).symm
    have key : ∀ (z : (↑(M.obj (op W)) : Type u)),
        (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
            ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf W x hxW)) z)
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf W x hxW))
            ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z) := by
      intro z
      rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op W))) z
            = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op W))) z from
            (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op W) z).symm]
      exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
        W x hxW ((toPresheaf _).map g) z
    rw [hr, hs, ← PresheafOfModules.germ_smul M x W hxW, key, map_smul,
        PresheafOfModules.germ_smul N x W hxW, key]

/-- **Germ characterisation of `stalkLinearMap`.** On the germ of a section `s` over
an open `U ∋ x`, `stalkLinearMap g x` is the germ of `g.app (op U) s`. This is the
defining naturality of the stalk map, exposed for the downstream d.2 assembly
(identifying the stalk map of `F ◁ g` with `id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x`). -/
lemma stalkLinearMap_germ
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
    (U : Opens X) (hx : x ∈ U) (s : (↑(M.obj (op U)) : Type u)) :
    stalkLinearMap g x ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s)
      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ N.presheaf U x hx))
          ((ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s) := by
  change (ConcreteCategory.hom ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map
        ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
      ((ConcreteCategory.hom (TopCat.Presheaf.germ M.presheaf U x hx)) s) = _
  rw [show (ConcreteCategory.hom (g.app (op U))) s
        = (ConcreteCategory.hom (((toPresheaf _).map g).app (op U))) s from
        (toPresheaf_map_app_apply g (op U) s).symm]
  exact TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply (F := M.presheaf) (G := N.presheaf)
    U x hx ((toPresheaf _).map g) s

/-- **A stalkwise-iso morphism induces a bijective `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map.**
If the underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, then `stalkLinearMap
g x` is bijective — hence (being `R.stalk x`-linear) an `R.stalk x`-linear
equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the form ingredient (d.1) feeds into the
`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step (tensoring an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence by `id` stays an
equivalence, flatness-free). -/
lemma stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
    Function.Bijective (stalkLinearMap g x) := by
  change Function.Bijective ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
    ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g)))
  exact ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso _

end StalkLinearMap

end PresheafOfModules

/-- **The `R.stalk x`-linear stalk equivalence of a stalkwise-iso morphism.** When the
underlying Ab-stalk map of `g` at `x` is an isomorphism, `stalkLinearMap g x` upgrades
to an `R.stalk x`-linear equivalence `M_x ≃ₗ N_x`. This is the exact object the route-(e)
`id_{F_x} ⊗ g_x` step consumes: tensoring it by `id_{F_x}` (`LinearEquiv.lTensor`) yields
an equivalence with no flatness hypothesis. -/
noncomputable def stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso
    {M N : PresheafOfModules.{u} (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)} (g : M ⟶ N) (x : X)
    (h : IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor AddCommGrpCat.{u} x).map ((toPresheaf _).map g))) :
    (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x) : Type u) ≃ₗ[↑(R.stalk x)]
      (↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk N.presheaf x) : Type u) :=
  LinearEquiv.ofBijective (stalkLinearMap g x) (stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso g x h)

end StalkLinearMap

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (4, all axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`):**
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap` — the `R.stalk x`-linear stalk map of a
    `PresheafOfModules` morphism (new section `StalkLinearMap`).
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap_germ` — its germ characterisation.
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso` — bijectivity from Ab-stalk iso.
  - `PresheafOfModules.stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso` — bundled `≃ₗ[R.stalk x]` version.
- **Declarations blocked (0 new):** the lone target `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`
  (L411) remains a typed `sorry`; its body comment now carries the corrected decomposition.
- **sorry count: 4 → 4** (unchanged): L411 `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (target,
  route-(e) residual), L901 `tensorObj_restrict_iso`, L1009 `exists_tensorObj_inverse`,
  L1049 `addCommGroup_via_tensorObj` (all three off-path, untouched). **No new sorries.**

## Step 0 — make-or-break existence check (RESOLVED: negative, must build)
Searched Mathlib for an off-the-shelf `MorphismProperty.IsMonoidal` on the module
sheafification localizer / monoidal `SheafOfModules` / `IsLocalization` glue.
- `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Monoidal.lean`: `(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal` exists but only
  for a **fixed** monoidal-closed `A` (`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`), via internal-hom adjunction — needs
  `MonoidalClosed A`. **Inapplicable**: `PresheafOfModules R` (varying ring) is not `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`,
  and `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R)` is verified-absent.
- `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/Point/IsMonoidalW.lean`: `(J.W (A := A)).IsMonoidal` via enough
  points — again fixed `A`. **Inapplicable** for the same reason; it IS the technique to port.
- No monoidal `SheafOfModules`; module-sheaf `Localization.lean` has no `IsMonoidal`.
- **Verdict:** no collapse available — `(J.W).IsMonoidal` for the module localizer must be built.

## KEY CORRECTION to PROGRESS.md / recipe (high value for planner)
PROGRESS.md and `analogies/ts-monoidalloc214.md` state "no `PresheafOfModules`
stalk/fiber/point infra (only `…/Presheaf/ColimitFunctor.lean`)". **This is WRONG.**
`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean` (Andrew Yang, 2026) already supplies, for
`X : TopCat`, `R : X.Presheaf CommRingCat`, `M : PresheafOfModules (R ⋙ forget₂ _ _)`:
- `instance : Module (R.stalk x) ↑(TopCat.Presheaf.stalk M.presheaf x)` (the stalk module);
- `PresheafOfModules.germ_smul` (germ / scalar compatibility).
Plus `Presheaf/ColimitFunctor.lean` has `colimitFunctor : PresheafOfModules R ⥤ ModuleCat cR.pt`.
This substantially de-risks ingredient (d.1): the module stalk does NOT need to be built.

## isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W (L411, target) — PARTIAL (d.1 core built)
- **Approach:** route (e) stalkwise. A `J.W`-morphism `g` is a stalkwise iso, so
  `(F ◁ g)_x = id_{F_x} ⊗_{R_x} g_x` is an iso for arbitrary `F` (flatness-free). Needs
  (d.1) module-`J.W` ⟺ stalkwise-iso, and (d.2) stalk ⊗ commutation.
- **Built this iter (d.1 core, axiom-clean):** `stalkLinearMap` (germ-chase via
  `germ_exist` + `germ_res_apply` + `germ_smul` + `stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply` +
  `toPresheaf_map_app_apply`), `stalkLinearMap_germ`, `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso`,
  `stalkLinearEquivOfIsIso`. These package the R_x-linear stalk equivalence the
  `id ⊗ g_x` step consumes.
- **Two residual gaps to close the sorry (precise):**
  1. **(d.1-bridge)** For the topological site, `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W
     ((toPresheaf _).map f) ↔ ∀ x, IsIso ((stalkFunctor Ab x).map ((toPresheaf _).map f))`.
     Routes: (a) `HasEnoughPoints (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)` (EXISTS:
     `Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/Points.lean:67`) + `ObjectProperty.…W_iff`
     (`Sites/Point/Conservative.lean:109`) — but `presheafFiber ≅ TopCat.Presheaf.stalk`
     is the Mathlib TODO (Points.lean L18-22), still absent; (b) `WEqualsLocallyBijective`
     + `Topology/Sheaves`: `locally_surjective_iff_surjective_on_stalks` (LocallySurjective.lean:80)
     and `app_injective_iff_stalkFunctor_map_injective` / sheaf-level
     `isIso_iff_stalkFunctor_map_iso` (Stalks.lean:512/652), bridging the site's
     `Presheaf.IsLocallyInjective/Surjective J` to the topological `TopCat.Presheaf` versions.
     Est. ~80–150 LOC.
  2. **(d.2)** Natural iso `(F ⊗ᵖ_R M).presheaf.stalk x ≅ F_x ⊗_{R.stalk x} M_x` identifying
     `(F ◁ g)_x` with `LinearMap.lTensor F_x (stalkLinearMap g x)`. "Tensor commutes with the
     filtered colimit defining the stalk" over the **varying** ring — genuinely Mathlib-absent,
     the largest piece (~150–250 LOC). Once it lands: `stalkLinearMap_bijective_of_isIso` +
     `LinearEquiv.lTensor` finish flatness-free.
- **Restructure required:** this lemma is stated over a GENERAL site `C` (no stalks exist there).
  It must be **specialised to `C = Opens X`** (or add `[HasEnoughPoints J]` + topological
  hypotheses). The decl is **UNPROTECTED** (not in `archon-protected.yaml`), and the only
  consumer chain (`W_whiskerLeft/Right_of_W` → `tensorObj_assoc_iso`) already runs over
  `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, so specialising is compatible. NB under route (e) the
  hand-assembled `tensorObj_assoc_iso` is to be superseded by `LocalizedMonoidal` anyway.
- **Dead ends (do NOT retry):** section-level injectivity-alone (needs Tor₁/flatness);
  `MonoidalClosed (PresheafOfModules R)` route (absent); the fixed-base `Sites/Monoidal.lean`
  / `IsMonoidalW.lean` instances (varying ring ≠ `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A`).

## Informal agent
Called `archon-informal-agent.py --provider auto` (MOONSHOT key set) → **HTTP 401 Invalid
Authentication** (key invalid). Treated as unavailable; proceeded on on-disk Mathlib analysis.

## Why I stopped
**Partial progress.** 4 axiom-clean declarations added (the d.1 R_x-linear-stalk-map core:
`stalkLinearMap` + 3 companions), verified `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. The
target sorry is NOT closed: it requires the (d.1-bridge) site-`W`↔stalk characterisation and
(d.2) stalk-⊗ commutation over a varying ring — multi-iteration infrastructure (d.2 genuinely
Mathlib-absent). Per mathlib-build mode this is expected incremental progress, NOT the PROGRESS.md
"Reversal"/bottom-out trigger (a route exists; the module stalk turned out to be present in
Mathlib, de-risking d.1). I did not attempt d.1-bridge/d.2 to completion this session because
each is a bounded-but-large build that cannot be finished axiom-clean now, and partial versions
would introduce forbidden sorries.

## Next step (planner)
1. Specialise `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` to `Opens X` (unprotected) — or split a
   topological-site variant — to gain access to the stalk machinery.
2. Build (d.1-bridge) from `WEqualsLocallyBijective` + the TopCat stalk criteria, then (d.2)
   from `germ_exist` + filtered-colimit-tensor. Assemble with the 4 bricks now present.
3. Then package `(J.W).IsMonoidal` and instantiate `Localization.Monoidal.LocalizedMonoidal`
   (Step B), retiring the hand-assembled associator.

## Blueprint markers (for review agent)
- `lem:islocallyinjective_whisker_of_W` (`isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W`): still has
  `sorry` — proof NOT `\leanok`. The new bricks (`stalkLinearMap` etc.) are not blueprint-pinned;
  plan agent may wish to record them as the d.1 ingredients under `sec:tensorobj_route_e`.

description: Lane TS — Mathlib HAS a PresheafOfModules stalk (contradicts PROGRESS.md "no module stalk infra")
metadata:

---

`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Stalk.lean` (Andrew Yang, 2026) provides, for

assert "no PresheafOfModules stalk/fiber/point infra (only ColimitFunctor)". That is WRONG and

**How to apply:** d.1's R_x-linear stalk map is now BUILT in

Remaining to close the sorry: (d.1-bridge) `(Opens.grothendieckTopology X).W ↔ ∀x stalk-iso`
